title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov shares experience building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The session covers production agent architecture including core loop design, feedback mechanisms, long context handling, monitoring, and enterprise tooling patterns using Claude and Effect-TS.
writeup: Gabe De Mesa of OpenGov walks through how OG Assist, an AI agent serving thousands of state and local governments, was built and scaled in production. The talk covers production agent architecture: core loop design, feedback mechanisms, long context handling, monitoring, and enterprise tooling patterns, implemented with Claude and Effect-TS. The takeaways center on the practical engineering needed to run agents at enterprise scale, including observability and evaluation. It connects these patterns to a real deployment rather than a prototype.
